title: Cheilanthes aurantia
intro: Cheilanthes aurantia, also known as the Orange Lip Fern, is a species of fern native to Australia, characterized by its bright orange-red fronds and delicate, lacy texture. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, with reported health benefits including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues and protect against cellular damage. Cheilanthes aurantia contains a range of bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In herbal medicine, Cheilanthes aurantia is often prepared as a tea or infusion, or used in combination with other herbs in tinctures and salves to support digestive health and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Cheilanthes aurantia is classified within the family Pteridaceae, commonly known as the maidenhair or lace-leaf fern family. This species is a member of the genus Cheilanthes, which comprises approximately 200 species of ferns. Cheilanthes aurantia is further grouped into the tribe Cheilanthaeae. The classification of Cheilanthes aurantia is based on its morphological characteristics, such as the shape of its leaves and the arrangement of its fronds. This fern is native to the Americas, where it is often found growing on rocky outcrops.
